purpose: Canonical reference for juice/game-feel patterns to be applied automatically by ForgeFlow's game-generation pipeline. Each pattern has a directive (what to do), a Phaser-3 implementation hint (how to do it), and a reference to a primary source.

primary_sources.title: The Art of Screenshake
primary_sources.author: Jan Willem Nijman (Vlambeer)
primary_sources.summary: 31 progressive 'juice' steps applied to a top-down shooter prototype: bigger bullets, more bullets, less bullet speed, kickback, screenshake, hit animations, enemy explosions, permanence (corpses, casings, decals), gun delay, decoupled gun position, camera lerp, sleep-on-hit (hit-stop), HP juice, sound, music.

primary_sources.title: Juice It Or Lose It
primary_sources.author: Martin Jonasson and Petri Purho
primary_sources.summary: Breakout clone is gradually 'juiced' with tweens, color, scaling, particles, screen shake, sound layering and music. Defines juice as 'constant, bountiful user feedback' for trivial actions.

primary_sources.title: Game Feel: A Game Designer's Guide to Virtual Sensation
primary_sources.author: Steve Swink
primary_sources.summary: Defines game feel as the tactile, kinesthetic sense of manipulating a virtual object. Three building blocks: real-time control, simulated space, polish. Polish = visual/audio/haptic effects amplifying the interaction.

primary_sources.title: Designing Game Feel: A Survey
primary_sources.author: Pichlmair & Johansen
primary_sources.summary: Academic survey enumerating juice patterns across genres: hit-stop, camera shake, particles, squash/stretch, post-processing, layered audio.

primary_sources.title: Explosions In Vlambeer's Nuclear Throne
primary_sources.summary: Documents Nuclear Throne's permanence layers: bodies, bullet casings, level destruction. Dust ring (short-lived, communicates motion) vs smoke ring (lingers).

primary_sources.title: Analysis of Screenshake Types
primary_sources.summary: Random offset is the default and works fine. Diminishing-strength shake (strong peak, long tail) feels best. Horizontal-only shake suits side-on platformers.

primary_sources.title: Phaser 3 Camera + FX docs
primary_sources.summary: Phaser 3.60+ ships built-in postFX (bloom, glow, blur, shine, vignette, wipe, pixelate, displacement) plus camera shake/flash/fade as core APIs.

primary_sources.title: Vector Storm playtest 2026-05-05 (5-10h iterative session)
primary_sources.summary: Live playtest of a generated arena shooter that uncovered cumulative-wave skip pattern, world-themed enemy rosters (vs music swaps), weapon stack-bonus system, boss-telegraphed attacks, instant-zap weapons, save-gated wave skip, polished GameOver with redundant-input buttons, and concurrent-object hard caps.

juice_principles.name: camera_shake_on_impact
juice_principles.directive: Shake the camera on every meaningful impact (kill, hit-taken, explosion); scale duration + intensity by combo size or damage tier.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.cameras.main.shake(80, 0.005)  // ms, normalized intensity 0..1; for big hits use 200ms @ 0.015

juice_principles.name: hit_stop_freeze_frame
juice_principles.directive: Freeze the world for 50-150ms on big hits to sell weight. Never exceed 200ms.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.physics.world.timeScale = 0.05; this.tweens.timeScale = 0.05; this.time.delayedCall(80, () => { this.physics.world.timeScale = 1; this.tweens.timeScale = 1; });

juice_principles.name: particle_burst_on_state_change
juice_principles.directive: Emit a particle burst on every kill, pickup, level-up, door-open, and tier-transition. Bursts live <600ms and never obscure gameplay.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.add.particles(x, y, 'spark', { speed: {min:80, max:240}, lifespan: 400, quantity: 12, scale: {start:0.6, end:0}, blendMode:'ADD' }).explode(12);

juice_principles.name: score_popup_float_fade
juice_principles.directive: Every score gain spawns a text that floats up ~40px and fades over 600ms with eased motion.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: const t = this.add.text(x, y, '+'+pts, {fontFamily:'Arial', fontSize:'18px', color:'#fff', stroke:'#000', strokeThickness:3}).setOrigin(0.5); this.tweens.add({ targets:t, y:t.y-40, alpha:0, duration:600, ease:'Cubic.easeOut', onComplete:()=>t.destroy() });

juice_principles.name: damage_number_popup
juice_principles.directive: Floating damage numbers from each enemy hit (skip if 50+ enemies on screen — toggle off for bullet-hell).
juice_principles.phaser_hint: Same as score_popup but anchored to enemy.x,enemy.y - 20; use color tier (white=normal, yellow=crit, red=heavy).

juice_principles.name: background_pulse_on_wave
juice_principles.directive: Shift background hue or brightness as waves/levels escalate; pulse on beat in arcade games.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.tweens.addCounter({ from:0, to:1, duration:2000, yoyo:true, repeat:-1, onUpdate: t => this.cameras.main.setBackgroundColor( Phaser.Display.Color.Interpolate.ColorWithColor(c1,c2,1,t.getValue()) ) });

juice_principles.name: bloom_glow_on_neon
juice_principles.directive: Apply bloom to bullets, explosions, pickups and any 'neon' element. Use sparingly — only on bright/saturated objects, never on UI text.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: bullet.postFX.addBloom(0xffffff, 1, 1, 1.2, 1.5, 4);  // color, offsetX, offsetY, blurStrength, strength, steps
juice_principles.anti_pattern: Do NOT bloom the whole scene; only bright objects.

juice_principles.name: chromatic_aberration_on_big_impact
juice_principles.directive: Brief RGB-channel split on death, boss hit, or screen-clear bombs. Pulse in then out over 200ms.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: Use a custom post-pipeline or simulate via 3 stacked tinted copies offset by 1-2px on the X axis; tween offset 0->2->0 over 200ms.

juice_principles.name: anticipation_followthrough
juice_principles.directive: Squash before action, stretch during, settle after. Apply to jumps, attacks, projectile launches.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.tweens.chain({ targets:player, tweens:[ {scaleX:1.2, scaleY:0.8, duration:80}, {scaleX:0.85, scaleY:1.2, duration:120}, {scaleX:1, scaleY:1, duration:150, ease:'Back.out'} ] });

juice_principles.name: telegraph_then_impact
juice_principles.directive: Enemy attacks flash a warning shape (red zone, charge glow) for 200-500ms BEFORE damage lands.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: const tel = this.add.rectangle(x,y,w,h,0xff0000,0.3); this.tweens.add({targets:tel, alpha:{from:0.2,to:0.6}, yoyo:true, repeat:2, duration:120, onComplete:()=>{ tel.destroy(); spawnHitbox(); }});

juice_principles.name: audio_layer_intensity
juice_principles.directive: Music has 2-3 stems (drums, bass, melody). Combo/wave triggers add or remove stems via volume tween.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.sound.add('drums',{loop:true,volume:0}).play(); this.tweens.add({targets:drums, volume:0.8, duration:1000});

juice_principles.name: rate_pitch_modulation
juice_principles.directive: Multiplier ticks, combo counters, and pickup chimes rise in pitch as the streak grows. Reset on miss.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: sfx.play({ rate: 1 + Math.min(combo*0.05, 1.0), detune: combo*30 });

juice_principles.name: easing_on_ui
juice_principles.directive: Every UI motion uses an easing curve. Default to Back.out for popups, Cubic.out for fades, Elastic.out for celebrations.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.tweens.add({ targets:menu, scale:{from:0,to:1}, duration:280, ease:'Back.out(1.7)' });
juice_principles.anti_pattern: Linear motion on UI feels robotic and cheap.

juice_principles.name: death_slow_mo_fast_restart
juice_principles.directive: On player death: slow time to 10-25%, hold 600-1200ms, fast wipe to restart. Restart loads in <1s.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.physics.world.timeScale = 0.1; this.cameras.main.zoomTo(1.5, 800); this.time.delayedCall(1200, () => this.scene.restart());

juice_principles.name: damage_flash_red_overlay
juice_principles.directive: On player damage: full-screen red flash (alpha 0.3, 80ms). On enemy hit: 50ms white tint.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.cameras.main.flash(80, 255, 0, 0, false);  // player damage  enemy.setTint(0xffffff); this.time.delayedCall(50, ()=>enemy.clearTint());

juice_principles.name: tier_palette_shift
juice_principles.directive: On tier-up (level, biome, difficulty), shift the global palette. Color quantization sells progression.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.tweens.addCounter({from:0,to:1,duration:1500,onUpdate:t=>{ /* lerp tint of shared atlas frames */ }});

juice_principles.name: camera_zoom_punch
juice_principles.directive: On bombs / wave-clear / boss-defeat: zoom out 5-10%, hold 80ms, ease back to 1.0 over 200ms.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.cameras.main.zoomTo(0.92, 80, 'Cubic.easeOut'); this.time.delayedCall(120, () => this.cameras.main.zoomTo(1.0, 200, 'Back.easeOut'));

juice_principles.name: permanence_corpses_decals
juice_principles.directive: Killed enemies leave bodies, casings, blood splats or charred craters that stay until level end. Sells player agency.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: Spawn a static sprite into a 'decals' RenderTexture on each kill rather than as a live sprite — keeps fill-rate flat.

juice_principles.name: kickback_recoil
juice_principles.directive: Player nudges backward 2-6px on shoot/hit. Sells weapon weight without changing input.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: this.tweens.add({ targets:player, x:player.x - 4*facing, duration:60, yoyo:true, ease:'Quad.out' });

juice_principles.name: diminishing_shake_curve
juice_principles.directive: Use a perlin-or-decay shake (strong peak, long soft tail) over flat random — same energy budget, much less fatigue.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: Phaser's built-in shake already decays linearly; for stronger effect run two shakes: shake(60, 0.012) + shake(180, 0.004) overlapping.

juice_principles.name: input_buffering_coyote
juice_principles.directive: Buffer jump/attack inputs 100-150ms before legality and grant 80-120ms coyote-time after leaving a ledge. Feels precise even on dropped frames.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: Track lastJumpPressedAt and lastGroundedAt; on any frame where (now - lastJumpPressedAt < 120 && now - lastGroundedAt < 100) execute jump.

juice_principles.name: cumulative_wave_skip_only_boss_clears
juice_principles.directive: In wave-based games, the natural wave timer expiring (or a player-triggered N-skip) should NOT clear existing enemies — leftover enemies stack into the next wave. Only a real boss-clear earns a screen wipe. This makes skips a real difficulty escalation (they 'stack' enemies) rather than a free reset.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: _advanceWave() { const isBossClear = this._isBossClearAdvance === true; this._isBossClearAdvance = false; if (isBossClear) { gameState.enemies.slice().forEach(e => this._killEnemy(e)); gameState.enemies = []; if (this.enemyGroup) this.enemyGroup.clear(true, false); /* + clear damage rings, enemy bullets, black holes */ } /* timer/skip advances skip the cleanup block */ }

juice_principles.name: world_themed_enemy_rosters
juice_principles.directive: Convey 'world' identity via enemy species changes, not music swaps. Each world (group of N waves) gets a distinct enemy roster with different visuals + AI behaviors. Music can stay constant the whole game; the player's eye reads the world transition from the new shapes/colors that arrive.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: _availableEnemyTypes(wave) { if (wave <= 3) return ['blue_square','diamond','snake','shooter']; if (wave <= 6) return ['squid','squiggle','jelly','waver']; return ['crystal','glitcher','cluster','gravity_well']; }

juice_principles.name: weapon_stack_bonus_system
juice_principles.directive: Weapon-upgrade pickups should reward DUPLICATES instead of being wasted. Picking up the same weapon you already have should: (a) extend the duration by N seconds, (b) increment a stack counter (cap 4), (c) grant a stack-scaled bonus per fire (extra projectile, +damage, wider spread, more chains). New tier resets stacks. Display stack count on HUD.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: _grantWeaponUpgrade(tier, durationMs=9000) { const sameTier = (this.weaponTier === tier && now < this.weaponTierExpiresAt); if (sameTier) { this.weaponStacks = Math.min(this.weaponStacks + 1, 4); this.weaponTierExpiresAt += durationMs; gameState.score += 250 * this.weaponStacks; } else { this.weaponTier = tier; this.weaponStacks = 0; this.weaponTierExpiresAt = now + durationMs; } }

juice_principles.name: boss_telegraphed_attacks
juice_principles.directive: Every boss attack should telegraph 0.4-0.6s before it fires. Common telegraph patterns: (a) brief scale-pulse on the boss graphics (1.0 → 1.4, yoyo), (b) bright color pulse, (c) tracer line preview for aimed shots. Without telegraphs, players feel cheated; with them, the boss feels readable and 'fair-hard'.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: if (time > enemy.lastShock - 500 && !enemy._shockTelegraphed) { enemy._shockTelegraphed = true; this.tweens.add({ targets: enemy.graphics, scale: 1.4, duration: 450, yoyo: true, ease: 'Cubic.easeOut' }); } if (time > enemy.lastShock) { this._emitDamageRing(enemy.x, enemy.y, color, radius); enemy.lastShock = time + cooldown; enemy._shockTelegraphed = false; }

juice_principles.name: concurrent_object_hard_caps
juice_principles.directive: At high-stack/high-multiplier moments, gameplay objects can grow unbounded (bullets, enemy bullets, score popups, chain bolts, enemies). Apply hard caps with oldest-first eviction. The player won't notice missing extras at the cap — but they'll definitely notice the freeze without caps.

juice_principles.name: instant_zap_weapon_no_projectile
juice_principles.directive: Some weapons feel better as INSTANT zaps from player to nearest enemy — no projectile travel, immediate visual + damage. Best fit: chain-lightning, laser, beam weapons. Implementation: skip the bullet system, find nearest enemy in forward arc, draw zigzag bolt directly, apply damage instantly, recurse for chain effects.

juice_principles.name: save_gated_wave_skip
juice_principles.directive: Wave-skip ('N' key) should be gated by a localStorage 'maxWaveReached' record — players can only skip TO a wave they've previously REACHED. This prevents fresh-save players from blasting to the final boss; rewards persistence. Save the high-water mark on every wave entry. UI: show 'Max Wave Reached: N' on the menu, with a 'Reset Save' option.

juice_principles.name: polished_gameover_with_redundant_input
juice_principles.directive: The GameOver screen MUST work on first click — players who lost a great run will rage-quit if 'TRY AGAIN' doesn't respond. Use Rectangle-based buttons (NOT Text+backgroundColor) AND a scene-level pointerdown listener AND a native DOM canvas listener AND keyboard ENTER/SPACE/R fallback. Reset any de-bounce flags on every create() call so re-entering GameOver works.
juice_principles.phaser_hint: // See research_phaser_patterns.json#text_setinteractive_unreliable_hit for full pattern. Game-over create() should reset this._goneToScene = false at the top, set up Rectangle buttons via _makeButton(), add scene-level input.on('pointerdown') with hit-test, add canvas DOM mousedown handler (with shutdown cleanup), and bind ENTER/SPACE to retry.

platformer: anticipation_followthrough (squash on jump, stretch in air, squash on landing)
particle_burst_on_state_change (run dust, jump dust, ground-pound shockwave)
camera_shake_on_impact (heavy landings + ground-pounds only, never on every step)
input_buffering_coyote (the platformer juice that lives in feel, not visuals)
audio_layer_intensity (footsteps shift surface SFX; combat layer fades in)
shmup: bullet trails (additive-blend particle stream behind every projectile)
damage_flash_red_overlay (on player; white tint on boss)
audio_layer_intensity (boss arrival fanfare, music intensifies at low boss HP)
camera_shake_on_impact (only on player hit and boss death)
flight: engine_glow (postFX bloom on thruster sprite, intensity tied to throttle)
camera_tilt_on_bank (camera roll 5-10 deg following player input, not raw)
audio_layer_intensity (engine pitch tracks throttle; combat stem on lock-on)
tier_palette_shift (sky color shifts per altitude band)
rpg: camera_zoom_punch (zoom in on encounter start; zoom out on victory)
easing_on_ui (menus open with Back.out, close with Cubic.in)
arpg: particle_burst_on_state_change (every ability has its own particle shape + color)
tier_palette_shift (loot rarity = white/blue/yellow/orange beam-of-light + sound tier)
hit_stop_freeze_frame (60-100ms on heavy attacks, 0 on light)
diminishing_shake_curve (scale by ability tier, not per-tick of DoT)
puzzle: easing_on_ui (snap-to-grid uses Back.out; pieces overshoot then settle)
particle_burst_on_state_change (match cascade emits 1 burst per chain step)
score_popup_float_fade (per match, scaled by chain length)
audio_layer_intensity (chain reaction adds drums; resets on no-move)
strategy: camera_zoom_punch (dramatic zoom on unit-killed or critical hit)
easing_on_ui (cards/menus use elastic; counters tween rather than snap)
simulation: easing_on_ui (placement snap with Back.out; satisfying click-into-place)
score_popup_float_fade (money +/- floats up green/red; experience bar tweens)
audio_layer_intensity (day-tick chime; ambient layer adds populace-noise as town grows)
rate_pitch_modulation (UI clicks rise in pitch on streak buys)
adventure: easing_on_ui (cinematic camera moves use ease-in-out, not lerp)
telegraph_then_impact (door opens after pause; chest opens with anticipation)
audio_layer_intensity (item-get pause + jingle, world music ducks)
dialogue typewriter + ducking music while text appears

modern_indie_references.signature: Cast-frame hit-flash + screen-shake gated by Aspect, plus boon-pickup zoom and god-voice chime. Screenshake is intense enough that the team ships an explicit accessibility toggle.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Every god boon = unique color + voice + UI takeover. Pickups are events, not menu items.

modern_indie_references.signature: Combo escalation: each successful hit ramps the combat audio stem and adds a 1-frame hit-stop; weapon-specific impact VFX read clearly even at 12 enemies on-screen.
modern_indie_references.lesson: VFX must read at speed. 2D-from-3D pipeline gives consistent silhouettes that survive screen shake + particles.

modern_indie_references.signature: Number-soup damage popups + screen-fill weapon effects + EXP-gem 'ching' chorus. So loud the dev added a damage-number toggle when fans complained.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Density is the juice. Every gem-pickup ching layered together becomes the music.

modern_indie_references.signature: Item-pickup fanfare + tier-color shift (white/green/red/yellow border) + layered sound stinger per tier. Stacking triggers cascading screen-fill.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Color-coded rarity + escalating audio = the player learns the rarity system without reading.

modern_indie_references.signature: 3 layers of permanence (corpses, casings, decals) + dust ring (motion) vs smoke ring (lingering) + gun kickback + weapon-pickup pause.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Permanence = retroactive proof of player agency. Cheap to ship if you bake decals into a RenderTexture.

modern_indie_references.signature: Whole-screen monochrome palette per run + chunky low-res spritework + heavy weighty kickback on the plane + bouncy water.
modern_indie_references.lesson: A single global palette can be juice on its own — palette swap = whole new game-feel.

modern_indie_references.signature: Bloom on every neon stroke, bullet trails, particle storm on death, on-beat enemy spawns synced to music.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Vector neon games live or die by bloom + additive blending. No bloom = student project.

modern_indie_references.signature: Dash freeze-frame (~2 frames) + particle burst + camera-no-shake (game uses input precision over camera juice). Coyote time + jump buffer set the platformer standard.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Hit-stop on the PLAYER's actions (dash) is as juicy as on enemies. Skip shake when you want the player to feel in control.

modern_indie_references.signature: Shield-impact freeze-frame + sword-arc trail particles + sub-second camera bump on heavy enemies; otherwise restrained.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Restraint is juice too. Save the big effects for the moments that earn them.

modern_indie_references.signature: Damage number tornado + per-weapon SFX layering + simple pulse on the character on hit; 60-second pacing with stat fanfare on shop entry.
modern_indie_references.lesson: Even a 1-screen autoshooter can feel premium if pickups, levelups and shop transitions each get their own audio + particle signature.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Constant shake -> motion sickness, accessibility complaint, players turn it off. Hades shipped a toggle because of this.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Shake only on discrete events. Never tie shake to a continuous variable like 'fire-held'.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: >200ms feels like the game froze. Players think it's a bug.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Cap hit-stop at 150ms. Use 50-80ms for normal hits, 120ms for boss/heavy.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Bloom-on-everything washes out the scene, kills text legibility, and makes every game look identical.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Apply bloom per-object only on bright/saturated sprites. Never on UI, never on the whole camera.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Vampire Survivors at endgame is the cautionary tale — players couldn't see their character.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Cap particle alpha + lifetime; particles must self-extinguish in <800ms. Add a 'reduce VFX' toggle if entity counts can exceed 200.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Without sidechain ducking, simultaneous SFX become noise; the cymbal never lands.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Side-chain SFX bus on important events; cap concurrent voices (Phaser default 32 is too many — use 8-12).

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Disorienting; loses the meaning of the zoom punch.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Reserve zoom for: bombs, wave-clears, boss-deaths. Once per ~30s ceiling.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Linear tweens feel cheap and robotic. No commercial polished game uses linear UI motion.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Default UI ease = Back.out for entrances, Cubic.in for exits.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Constant music + SFX = nothing feels important. Quiet moments make loud moments hit.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Drop music to 30% on boss intro, restore on first hit. Use 0.4s of silence before a phase change.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Permanent CA reads as 'broken display' and creates eye-strain in 30 seconds.
anti_juice_warnings.rule: CA is a punctuation mark, not a paragraph. Pulse-only on major impacts, max 200ms.

anti_juice_warnings.why_bad: Number soup obscures gameplay (Vampire Survivors lesson).
anti_juice_warnings.rule: Auto-disable damage numbers when entity count > threshold; or aggregate ('x12') instead of per-hit.

phaser_3_implementation_recipes.notes: Phaser's shake decays linearly. Stack two for diminishing-strength curve: shake(60, 0.012); then shake(180, 0.004).
Phaser arcade physics uses INVERTED timeScale: 1 = normal, higher = slower. Tweens use 1=normal, 0=paused.
setDepth(1000) keeps popup above gameplay sprites.
blendMode:'ADD' is essential for neon/spark feel. Auto-destroys after lifespan.
setTintFill (not setTint) gives the flat white-out look used by Hades / Dead Cells.
Combine with camera.flash() and camera.shake() for full bomb feel.
Sprite must have origin set to bottom-center for landing squash to read correctly.
Renders into a single texture — keeps fill-rate flat regardless of decal count.

pipeline_application_notes: For every game generated by the pipeline, assert the following minimum juice baseline before shipping: camera_shake_on_impact, hit_stop_freeze_frame, particle_burst_on_state_change, score_popup_float_fade, easing_on_ui, audio_layer_intensity. These six are the floor.
Additional juice is selected from per_genre_juice_priorities[<genre>] — pick the top 3-5 for that genre.
All numerical values come from default_tunables_reference_table — never invent timings.
Always wire an accessibility toggle for screen shake (boolean: shakeEnabled) — Hades shipped one for a reason.
Bloom and chromatic aberration are OFF by default; enable per-object only when sprite is flagged 'neon' or on explicit boss-death events.
